frame_analyses.description: Title slide: 'HOW TO USE KNOT PUSHER' by DR NG YING SHAN
Overhead view showing Bhandarkar knot pusher with reducer component. Yellow arrow indicates reducer, blue arrow indicates knot pusher body.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instructional diagram showing proper tool anatomy and nomenclature

frame_analyses.description: Closeup of knot pusher held in hand, demonstrating grip and orientation
Hands demonstrating formation of a suture loop held between fingers
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows how to create working loop for knot formation

frame_analyses.description: Closeup showing reverse loading technique with knot pusher approaching a blue loop on suture
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates how to load the knot pusher reducer onto the formed knot

frame_analyses.description: Instructor demonstrating how to position and hide the loop within the knot pusher
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows proper positioning of loop to prepare for knot advancement

frame_analyses.description: Hands holding suture thread with knot pusher, yellow arrow indicating thread control direction
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates maintaining tension on suture thread while managing knot pusher

frame_analyses.description: Transition to FLS trainer setup showing actual Penrose drain with marked targets and knot pusher ready to introduce knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows actual task setup with light-blue illumination of trainer box

frame_analyses.description: Overhead view of trainer with formed knot visible on suture, knot pusher positioned to advance knot toward drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates proper introduction of extracorporeal knot into trainer

frame_analyses.description: Close-up inset showing detail of knot being tightened on Penrose drain with suture marks visible. Main view shows full trainer setup.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows advancing the knot pusher to apply tension and tighten knot on drain

frame_analyses.description: Close-up detail view showing knot compression on drain with proper tension application
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates progressive tightening of knot around marked closure site

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher being withdrawn from trainer after knot has been tightened on drain. Inset shows completed knot securing the slit.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows proper withdrawal technique after knot placement and tightening

video_classification_reasoning: This is an educational/instructional video narrated with text overlays and directed by Dr Ng Ying Shan. The content includes title slides, equipment diagrams, technique demonstrations on a tabletop model, and explicit instructional labels (e.g., 'Reverse load', 'Hold the thread', 'Introduce & push the knot', 'Loop & adjust', 'Push to tighten', 'Withdraw'). This is NOT a trainee performance being evaluated; it is a tutorial on proper knot pusher technique. Do NOT score as a performance.

score_components.reason: Video classified as instructional/educational content, not a performance evaluation

demonstrated_techniques: Proper knot pusher (Bhandarkar) anatomy and component identification
Correct grip and hand positioning for knot pusher tool
Extracorporeal knot formation in free space prior to introduction
Loop formation and control using finger technique
Reverse loading of knot pusher reducer onto formed loop
Positioning and hiding the loop within the knot pusher for introduction
Suture thread tension management during knot advancement
Introduction of extracorporeal knot into trainer box
Advancement of knot pusher to tighten knot on Penrose drain
Proper withdrawal of knot pusher after knot placement
common_errors_addressed: Proper tool identification to avoid confusion between pusher body and reducer
Correct direction of loop loading to prevent knot misalignment
Maintaining thread tension to prevent premature knot unwinding
Gradual advancement of knot pusher to ensure proper slit closure without over-tightening
Complete withdrawal of pusher after knot tightening to verify knot security

confidence_rationale: Video is clearly instructional with explicit labeling and educational structure. No performance evaluation is possible or appropriate. Content demonstrates expert-level technique instruction with clear visual demonstrations and text cues.
cannot_determine: Actual task penalties (instructional demonstration, not evaluated task)
summary: This is a teaching video by Dr Ng Ying Shan on proper knot pusher technique for extracorporeal suturing in FLS. The video systematically covers tool anatomy, loop formation, reverse loading, and knot introduction/tightening techniques. It is NOT a trainee performance and should NOT be scored using the FLS rubric. It is valuable instructional content demonstrating expert technique.
